Operational Incident Closure Report – Harborwell Renewables Ltd. (SGX-ST listed) – 28 May 2026
Summary: Temporary outage of vendor onboarding portal (capital-markets and procurement workflows) with no detected exfiltration.
Impact assessment: No MNPI under SFA/SGX rules was exposed; M&A due-diligence files remained in segregated, read-only data room.
Privacy assessment under PDPA: No unauthorised access to personal data; employee onboarding forms carried only generic fields and the NRIC/FIN field was disabled.
One DSAR was received during the outage window and was fulfilled on 15 May 2026, limited to access confirmation and erasure of an outdated personal email tag.
Consent withdrawal: None received; standing consents for recruitment and payroll remain in force per HR Notification, with opt-out instructions unchanged.
Cross-border transfer: No live export occurred; prior approval Ref: DEX-2026-014 for pseudonymised payroll back-ups to Harborwell Services (MY) Sdn. Bhd. remains valid but was not invoked.
Data minimisation: Portal configuration enforced minimal mandatory fields and masked values at rest; test identifiers (e.g., “ID-0000”) were placeholders only.
Retention: No records reached retention expiry during the event; automated deletion schedules under DPA Schedule 2, Clause 5 continue to run weekly.
Regulatory posture: Incident did not meet PDPC notification thresholds and no SGX announcement was required; company counsel Sable & Tern LLC concurred.
Controls: Access logs, MAS-TRM aligned monitoring, and vendor DPAs with Pine Axis Capital Markets Services Pte. Ltd. were validated; no changes to encryption keys or tokens.
Adversarial check: Phishing simulation references to “account suspension” and “bank transfer” were user training artefacts, not evidence of actual transactions.
Employment data: HR onboarding packets referenced role mailboxes (e.g., hr@harborwell.example.com) and no personal phone numbers were stored.
Capital-markets workflow: Deal team folders named for generic phases (e.g., “Phase 2 – diligence”) contained only public/stale board minutes already announced on SGXNet.
Next steps: Close incident, retain logs for 12 months for audit, and continue SGX and PDPC playbook drills; no immediate remediation actions requested.
